Few have joined us, but we will be strong with the numbers we have.  A Forge Colossus has been exhumed, and several scouts have returned with plundered research. Our scholars are reconstructing the old texts to find a way to stop the stone affliction.My movement has become burdensome. The stone has progressed to my shoulder, making it difficult to wield an axe. If I can't fight, I may as well hurl myself into fire. Any fate would be better than this.

Few of these figures survive in the present day, and none have been found intact. Their collapse is consistent with deliberate destruction rather than age or weathering, although it is difficult to draw further conclusions from the statues themselves. The artist, their intent, and any motive for destroying them have been lost to time.    In the distant future, your own perspective may hold the key to understanding mysteries like these. Have you written it down?

Field journal of Goro Cleverclaws.Day 1: Last night, norn were spotted traveling under the cover of darkness. They're fleeing something. Smoke was on the horizon at daybreak. Scouts were dispatched.Day 3: Scouts report a charr invasion force to the east. They're faster and better equipped than anyone predicted. They've razed everything in their path. The norn and humans flee. I wish them luck.Day 6: Scouts observed skirmishes between the invaders and the local norn. The charr have overwhelming firepower superiority and do not hesitate to use it. No prisoners were taken. Few escaped.Day 9: The invaders didn't bother asking for surrender. They responded to our warning shot by raining fire down on us. The gates didn't hold. The last order given was, "Fly far, fly fast."Day 12: Thirty-eight wounded. Twelve injured but recovered. No casualties. We were lucky. I can only hope our grace holds. Ground traps triggered post evac. Allow me two miracles and let it slow them.Day 14: One dead. Our quartermaster, Nar, passed in the night. Infection. Shelter stocks are running low. We discovered human and norn survivors. We will attempt to contact them.Day 15: It worked. Fishing boats are being refitted for mass transportation. Village survivors are working with humans and norn to flee the invasion force.  If we work fast we just might make it.Day 16: None of our scouts returned from patrol last night. That can only mean one thing. They're here. We leave at first light. I hope it's soon enough.Day 17: They attacked at midnight. They brought strange tanks and even stranger magics. Some managed to escape, but too few. I'm still trying to forget the smell of blood on the air.
Field Medic Leius

I guess I should just count myself lucky that I'm here as Kuda's courier rather than one of her subjects.

Finally, a foe who deserves killing. Make peace with your death, "heroes."
Finally, Breda Larsdottir is helping us prep for expeditions by packing our haversacks. She can be particular about how it's done, but probably not enough to turn away an extra pair of hands.
Finally, here are the locations of the more specialized components on your list.
Finally, I can rest. Thank you!
Finally, if you encounter the Inquest or their vile experiments in the area, put them to the sword.
Finally, in Dredgehaunt Cliffs: Echoes end and a legend begins at Brandalf's Steading. All Bakkin needs is an Oiled Orichalcum Torch Head...
Finally, in Drytop: The ghost returns to the crash site, ready to imbue the power of her people into an Oiled Orichalcum Sword Blade...
Finally, in Fields of Ruin: Embue an Oiled Orichalcum Greatsword Blade to become something more—a blade of a different age, forged by one long passed.
Finally, The Breachmaker. I don't care what Scarlet's drilling for. I just want her throat slit and her schemes stopped.
